//: Five profiles for inspecting the MCP request/response cycle. Pick by need:
//1: 'MCP: chat (normal run)'         - just run the demo, no debugger overhead.
//2: 'MCP: chat (debug client)'       - break in main.py, mcp_client.py, core/*.
//3: 'MCP: chat (wire-trace server)'  - see every JSON-RPC frame in DEBUG CONSOLE + logs/.
//4: 'MCP: server only (attach)'      - debug the server in isolation (run server first).
//5: 'MCP: server only (debugpy)'     - launch server with debugpy waiting on :5678.

configurations.name: MCP: chat (debug client)
configurations.//: justMyCode=false lets you step into mcp.client.session and watch the SDK negotiate the wire protocol.

configurations.name: MCP: chat (wire-trace server)
configurations.//: MCP_WIRE_TRACE=1 makes main.py spawn scripts/mcp_wire_trace.py instead of the bare server. Every JSON-RPC frame is mirrored to stderr (visible in the integrated terminal) and to logs/mcp_wire_trace.log.

configurations.name: MCP: server only (debugpy)
configurations.//: Useful for setting breakpoints inside @mcp.tool / @mcp.prompt bodies. Pair with 'MCP: server only (attach)' if you want to drive it from a separate client process.

configurations.name: MCP: server only (attach)
configurations.//: Attach to a server that was started with: python -m debugpy --listen 5678 --wait-for-client mcp_server.py

compounds.name: MCP: full stack (client + wire trace)
compounds.//: Single-profile compound today; placeholder for adding a parallel server-debugpy attach later if you want simultaneous breakpoints on both sides.
